# clearpass_enforcement_policies (Data Source)

Data source for retrieving a list of Enforcement Policies from ClearPass.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Retrieve all Enforcement Policies from ClearPass
data "clearpass_enforcement_policies" "all" {}

# Output the list of policies
output "all_enforcement_policies" {
  value = data.clearpass_enforcement_policies.all.policies
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Read-Only

- `policies` (Attributes List) List of Enforcement Policies. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--policies))

<a id="nestedatt--policies"></a>
### Nested Schema for `policies`

Read-Only:

- `default_enforcement_profile` (String) The profile applied as a fallback if none of the policy rules match the session conditions.
- `description` (String) A human-readable description of the Enforcement Policy.
- `enforcement_type` (String) The type of the Enforcement Policy indicating its application context (e.g., RADIUS, TACACS, WEBAUTH, Application, Event).
- `id` (Number) The numeric ID of the Enforcement Policy in ClearPass.
- `name` (String) The unique name of the Enforcement Policy.
- `rule_eval_algo` (String) The logic used to evaluate the rules. Typically 'first-applicable' (stops after the first match) or 'evaluate-all' (processes all rules).
- `rules` (Attributes List) The ordered list of conditional rules configured for the Enforcement Policy. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--policies--rules))

<a id="nestedatt--policies--rules"></a>
### Nested Schema for `policies.rules`

Read-Only:

- `condition` (Attributes List) The set of conditions that must be evaluated for this rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--policies--rules--condition))
- `enforcement_profile_names` (List of String) List of Enforcement Profile names that are applied when this rule's conditions are met.

<a id="nestedatt--policies--rules--condition"></a>
### Nested Schema for `policies.rules.condition`

Read-Only:

- `name` (String) The specific attribute name within the condition type (e.g., 'Calling-Station-Id', 'Role').
- `oper` (String) The operator used for comparison (e.g., 'EQUALS', 'CONTAINS', 'BELONGS_TO', 'MATCHES_REGEX').
- `type` (String) The namespace or category of the condition (e.g., 'Radius:IETF', 'Tips', 'Connection').
- `value` (String) The value against which the attribute is compared.
